title: NOC 12102 – Procurement and Purchasing Agents and Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Procurement and Purchasing Agents and Officers play a vital role in supply chain strategy, vendor negotiations, and cost control across organizations in Canada. Classified under NOC 12102, this occupation benefits from nationwide demand and offers strong eligibility for skilled immigration programs leading to Permanent Residence (PR).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Evaluate supply-market risks and prepare forecasts and sourcing scenarios
Conduct research for tendering and contract strategies
Optimize supplier networks and delivery timelines
Maintain relationships with vendors and buyers
Respond to internal client inquiries and clarify requirements
Purchase specialized equipment, materials, and business services
Invite tenders and assess supplier quotations
Negotiate contract terms and award agreements
Monitor delivery schedules and resolve supply issues
Negotiate land or access rights where required
employment_requirements: A bachelor’s degree or college diploma in business administration, commerce, or economics
Specialized purchasing roles may require engineering or technical education
Prior experience in purchasing or administration is often required
IRWA certification may apply for land acquisition roles
Sourcing specialists usually require degrees in logistics, operations, finance, engineering, or analytics
job_outlook: From 2022 to 2031, Canada is expected to generate 37,800 new positions for Procurement and Purchasing Agents and Officers, compared with approximately 38,200 new job seekers—making regional targeting and immigration strategy especially important for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Procurement and Purchasing professionals are actively recruited across both public institutions and private-sector enterprises, especially in infrastructure, manufacturing, energy, healthcare, and government procurement.

Post-pandemic expansion in sourcing and logistics rolesStable mid-skill to senior administrative careersEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration pathwaysAnnual earnings typically ranging from $41,700 to $98,400
Why Choose Canada as a Procurement Professional?
Universal public healthcareCompetitive compensation packagesStrong workplace protectionsWork–life balanceMulticultural communitiesClear routes from work permits to PR
